betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The inhabitants of Kashan, they possess the peculiar ability to mirror the state of the mind. Of a mind that is burdened by many yokes.
Yokes I was unable to see, and yet the Spren, through their innate ability, are capable of making them evident to my unseeing eyes.
My encounters with Fractallites, they left deep scars. 
And the deepest of them, my loss of faith. Even back on earth, I was never a strong believer, but I guess part of me still trusted; that things would turn out for the better.
As I underwent my journey through the mythic shells, that trust, instead, was turned inward, and turned into self-centeredness. I stopped believing, and instead I started acting. Alone, taking onto myself the burden of divinity, without acknowledging what I was doing.
But now, it is evident. Despite all my power, I am powerless in front of what really matters. My life is not my own.
I witnessed this in Vethea, and called it enlightment then, while still holding on to the resolution that only I, by myself, would be the one responsible for my destiny. 
But now I see how little, even as a Fractallite, I can do.
That is why the fields of divinity, the aspects of recursion are fragmented. No mere person can access them all. 
There must be faith, that the Mission is worth following.
There must be faith, that other Missions exist for a reason.
There must be faith, that the others will be faithful themselves.
